items.title: About
items.text: This is my main GitHub Page.

items.title: Bio
items.text: My name is Manfred Rosenboom (maroph). I worked as a software lead developer for Fujitsu Germany, mainly on the SecDocs Archive Service project.

items.title: Skills
items.text:
• Databases H2, Oracle DB/RAC, MariaDB, MySQL
  • Languages Dutch, English, German (native speaker)
  • Operating Systems Debian, SUSE SLES
  • Programming Languages Bash, C, Java, JavaScript, PHP, Python, SQL
  • Servers Apache Web Server, Tomcat, WildFly

You can contact me via

  • Mastodon
  • Personal Home Page

items.title: Global Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
items.text: This website does not collect, store or process personally identifiable information (PII). This website does not require the use of cookies.

items.title: Third Part Inclusion
items.text: This website is hosted by GitHub using GitHub Pages. The GitHub Global Privacy Practices are available here.

items.title: Datenschutzerklärung (DSGVO)
items.text: Auf dieser Webseite werden keine personenbezogenen Daten erhoben, gespeichert oder verarbeitet. Es werden keine Cookies verwendet.

items.title: Verweise auf Dritte
items.text: Diese Website wird auf GitHub als GitHub Pages gehostet. Die GitHub Global Privacy Practices sind hier hinterlegt.

items.title: This Site
items.text: This is my main GitHub Page. The data are stored in my GitHub repository maroph/maroph.github.io.

items.title: How I create the pages on this site
items.text: My pages are written in Markdown (directory docs) an transformed into HTML pages (local directory site) with the Zensical static site generator. The site data are published in the gh-pages branch of the directory.

items.title: Python modules in use to produce this site:
items.text:
• Zensical
  • ghp-import

items.title: Python Virtual Environment
items.text: I use a virtual environment with the above Python modules.

items.title: How the virtual environment is created
items.text:
The following commands are used to build.bash the needed virtual environment:

$ python3 -m venv ./venv
$ source ./venv/bin/activate
$ python -m pip install --upgrade pip
$ python -m pip install --upgrade setuptools
$ python -m pip install --upgrade wheel
$ python -m pip install --upgrade zensical
$ python -m pip install --upgrade ghp-import

items.title: Create the virtual environment with the build.bash script
items.text:
$ ./build.bash venv
$ source ./venv/bin/activate

items.title: Install missing Python3 software
items.text:
If pip for Python3 and/or the Python3 venv module aren't installed on your system use the following commands as administrator (root):

$ sudo apt install python3-pip
$ sudo apt install python3-venv

items.title: Build the docs
items.text:
$ source venv/bin/activate
$ zensical build

or

$ source venv/bin/activate
$ ./build.bash

items.title: Build the docs for testing
items.text:
$ source venv/bin/activate
$ ./build.bash serve
build.bash: check for needed Python modules
----------
Name: zensical
Version: 0.0.10

Name: ghp-import
Version: 2.1.0
----------

shutdown Zensical server: ./zensical.shut

items.title: Build the docs for publishing
items.text: The build step is done by the GitHub Actions file .github/workflows/ci.yml by every checkin in the branch main.

items.title: Manually: ghp-import deploy
items.text:
$ source venv/bin/activate
$ ./build.bash deploy

$ git commit -m "my commit message"
$ git push

items.title: Sources
items.text:
This site is build with Zensical.

The intro picture (assets/THHGTTG_intro.png) at the home page is a renamed copy of the image https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/images/ic/480xn/p01td1v2.jpg. This image is a screenshot of the original "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy" Infocom game released in 1984.

You can play a modernized version of this game at the following BBC site The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy - 30th Anniversary Edition
